description: Inside an apartment, Ross and Bonnie stand near each other. Ross says, "Hi, Bonnie."
They kiss on the cheek and hold hands. Bonnie says, "My boss sent me off early, so I took the train."
Bonnie turns toward Joey, Phoebe, and Chandler, who sit at a nearby table. Joey is shirtless. "What are you guys doing?" she asks. Chandler replies, "We're playing strip Happy Days game."
Bonnie laughs and looks back at Ross.
"Cool. I'll catch up," Bonnie says. She lifts her black sweater over her head.
Bonnie pulls her sweater off, revealing a light blue bra, and walks past Ross. The scene changes to Rachel standing in a modern kitchen.
An exterior view shows a sandy hill and a house next to the ocean under a low sun. The scene returns to the kitchen, where Chandler stands near Monica as she opens cereal boxes.
Chandler holds a small bottle and says to Monica, "So you still don't think I'm boyfriend material,"
Chandler continues looking at Monica.
Chandler gestures with his free hand and says, "huh? Saw you checking me out during the game last"
Chandler maintains his posture.
"even," Chandler finishes. Monica turns to him and says, "Take off your pants."
Chandler stares at Monica, his hands raised slightly.
Chandler points his bottle at her and says, "Yeah, well, lucky for you."
Chandler looks away and lowers his hands.
"What?" Monica asks. Chandler turns away and says, "I don't know."
Rachel walks into the kitchen wearing a floral dress.
Rachel spreads her arms wide and says, "Well, is everybody else having just the best time?" Phoebe steps into the kitchen behind her.
Phoebe steps close behind Rachel, touching Rachel's shoulder.
"Joey's asleep," Phoebe whispers softly.
The view shifts to the kitchen floor, revealing Joey lying asleep on his back, buried from the chest down in sand sculpted into a mermaid tail.
Chandler, Monica, Rachel, and Phoebe stand around Joey, looking down. Chandler points his finger at Joey.
Chandler looks up at Rachel and says, "After he passed out, we put the sand around him to keep him warm." Joey remains asleep in the sand.
Rachel walks past the group to the counter and pours orange juice from a jug into a glass.
Holding the jug, Rachel asks, "Well, I assume the happy couple isn't up yet? Did you guys hear them last night?" Chandler looks up and replies, "Oh, yeah."
Chandler gestures with the bottle and says, "I don't know what they were doing but at one point sea turtles actually came up to the house."
Rachel lifts her glass and drinks. Ross and Bonnie enter the kitchen from the hallway.
"Morning," Ross says. "Hey," Rachel replies, turning to face them.
"How'd everybody sleep?" Ross asks. Phoebe, turning toward him, and Chandler respond together: "Oh, great." "Like a log."
Ross and Bonnie turn toward each other and kiss. Rachel averts her gaze and sets down her glass.
"I'm going for a walk," Rachel says as she quickly walks past Joey and leaves the room.
Joey opens his eyes and lifts his head, his chest rising as he looks around.
Ross looks down at Joey and says, "Good morning. Nice breasts, by the way." Joey glances down at his sculpted sand breasts, his eyes widening.
Joey's eyes bulge as he stares down at the sand breasts. He then looks forward with his mouth slightly open.
The setting changes to an outdoor wooden deck. Rachel sits on a bench reading a book. Bonnie, wearing a black bikini top and denim shorts, approaches with a striped towel.
Rachel lowers her book as Bonnie approaches. "Hey, what happened to you?" Rachel asks.
"Oh, nothing. I just felt like hanging out here and reading," Rachel says. Bonnie rubs her wet hair with the towel and says, "The water was so great. We jumped off this pier and my"
"suit came off," Bonnie finishes, gesturing with one hand.
Rachel raises her eyebrows and tilts her head slightly.
"Sorry I missed that. God, Joey and Chandler sure are funny," Rachel says, looking up at Bonnie.
Bonnie runs her hand through her wet hair, pushing it back.
Bonnie says, "I think I brought back half the beach in my hair. So much easier when I used to shave my head."
Rachel drops her head down briefly, closes her book, and looks back up at Bonnie.
"You know, I gotta tell you, I just loved your look when you were bald," Rachel says. Bonnie smiles and asks, "Really?"
Bonnie turns slightly, keeping her gaze on Rachel.
"Because I think about shaving it all off again sometime," Bonnie says, gesturing with her hands.
Rachel nods slightly.
"Really?" Rachel asks, her voice rising in pitch.
Bonnie smiles and looks down briefly.

Rachel gestures with her hand and says, "I mean, you definitely should do that." Bonnie nods and says, "You know what? I should do it." "Yeah," Rachel agrees. Bonnie continues, "You know what? Thank you, Rachel. You are"
"so cool," Bonnie concludes. Rachel smiles, waves her hand dismissively, and says, "Oh, stop."
Bonnie turns to walk away.
Rachel pumps her fist and says, "Now, let's shave that head." Bonnie replies, "All right," as they both begin to leave the deck.
Rachel sits back down on the bench and opens her book, smiling broadly.
The scene transitions to a shot of the sun low over the water, then returns to the kitchen where Monica chops vegetables and Chandler stirs a salad bowl.
Chandler turns his head toward Monica and says, "I know I've asked you this before. A hundred times before."
Monica pauses her chopping, lifts her large knife, and points the blade toward Chandler. Chandler raises his hands slightly.
Chandler holds his fist near his mouth and asks, "How am I not right for you?" Monica replies, "I don't know."
Monica resumes chopping vegetables.
"I like guys like Richard," Monica says, continuing to chop.
Monica picks up a piece of vegetable and says, "Or Pete before he went insane."
Monica sets the vegetable down and says, "I don't know, somebody more mature." Chandler's voice rises slightly in volume as he asks, "Oh, so I'm not mature enough for you?"
Chandler crosses his eyes, contorts his face, and says, "Monica needs somebody more mature," his voice sounding tight and distorted.
Chandler maintains his contorted facial expression while looking at Monica. Monica stares back at him with an expressionless face.
In a kitchen with a sand-covered floor, Joey and Phoebe stand near Chandler and Monica. Phoebe sets down a stool. Joey asks, "What's the matter?"

Joey asks, "Phoebes?" Phoebe gestures rapidly and says, "She canceled on me. She canceled on me." Joey asks, "What?" "Yes. She claims she had to go out of town," Phoebe continues, as Rachel walks into the kitchen.
Phoebe points to herself and says, "Suddenly she's avoiding me."
Phoebe continues gesturing animatedly, "She doesn't want to tell me where my father is. She knows and she won't tell me." Rachel stands with her arms crossed.
Rachel says, "Phoebe, that sucks," while keeping her arms crossed.
Phoebe turns slightly, lowering her hands.
Phoebe says, "Yeah, well, don't 'Phoebe, that sucks' me yet," then turns and walks quickly toward the door.
Ross enters the room as Phoebe walks past him.
"Where are you going?" Ross asks, gesturing with his hand.

Phoebe grabs her green bag strap and says, "Well, she's out of town, so there's got to be something in her house that tells me where my father is." Ross leans against the wall and says, "Phoebe, some"
Ross finishes, "people call it breaking and entering." Phoebe gestures toward the others and asks, "Well, are any of those people?"
Chandler, Monica, and Joey quickly say, "No. No." while shaking their heads.
Phoebe nods and gestures widely with both hands.
"Okay, look, I'll do something nice, okay?" Phoebe says.
She points and says, "I'll fill her ice trays," then turns and walks out.
Ross points after her and says, "Good."
Ross turns his head toward the hallway.
A completely bald woman, Bonnie, enters the room holding a towel. "Hey, everybody," she says, smiling.
The group stares in shock. Rachel covers her mouth.
Someone says, "Wow,"
Ross steps forward, his eyes wide and mouth open. He stammers and gestures awkwardly toward Bonnie's head.
Ross continues to gesture toward her and says, "Look what you did."
Bonnie smiles widely and adjusts her towel. Ross stares at her head.
Bonnie smiles at Ross and asks, "You want to touch it?"
Ross shakes his head and says, "No, but it's great." Bonnie leans forward, laughing, and says, "Come on, touch it."
Bonnie leans her head toward Ross.
Ross says, "Okay," and slowly reaches his hand forward.
Ross places his palm flat on top of Bonnie's bare head.
Bonnie smiles and says, "You can feel all the bones in your skull." Ross rubs her head slightly. Rachel, Chandler, and Monica watch with uncomfortable facial expressions.
Ross pulls his hand back quickly, grimacing.
Ross says, "No." Bonnie laughs.
The setting changes to a night view of the moon reflecting on the ocean, then to a beach house deck where Rachel sits holding a glass.
Ross walks out the door, approaches Rachel, and places one foot on a wooden bench near her.
Ross gestures as he speaks to Rachel. "Having a little chat with Bonnie, and guess what? She happened to bring up you know who was behind the whole head shaving" he says.
Ross continues, "idea. And now, who was it?" pointing at Rachel.
Ross nods and says, "Oh, that's all right. That's right. It was you," pointing at her again.
Rachel lowers her glass and looks up at Ross.
Rachel raises one hand and says, "That was her idea."
Rachel gestures with both hands and says, "I just gave her a nudge." Ross replies, "She said you gave her the razor." Rachel smiles.
Bonnie steps out onto the deck wearing a bikini top and a towel, approaching Ross and Rachel.
Bonnie touches Ross's arm and says, "So anyone up for a midnight dip in the ocean?" Ross looks at her and replies, "I'm good."
Bonnie pats Ross's arm and says, "Okay. I'll see you in a bit."
Ross looks at Bonnie as she prepares to leave.
Ross says, "Okay. Have fun," as Bonnie walks away.
Ross turns back toward Rachel. Rachel stands up, crossing her arms.
Rachel, arms crossed, says, "She doesn't look that bad."
Ross turns to look out toward the beach.
Ross stares out toward the beach and says, "You can see the moonlight bouncing off her head."
Ross turns his head back to face Rachel.
Ross raises his hands and asks, "What the hell were you thinking?" Rachel shakes her head slightly and says, "I don't know."
Ross points at Rachel, his volume louder than his previous line, "You don't know? Rachel, you balded my girlfriend, all right?" Rachel turns and walks off the deck.
Ross follows Rachel to the sandy area next to a parked car. Rachel stops and turns around.
Rachel raises her hands and asks loudly, "Do you think it's easy for me to see you with somebody else?"
Ross points at her and says, "You know what? Hey, you're the one who ended it, remember?" Rachel steps closer, her volume rising, "Yeah, because I was mad at you. Not because I stopped loving you."
Ross stares at Rachel, his facial expression changing. Rachel looks back at him.
Ross lowers his volume, asking, "You still love me?" Rachel crosses her arms and says softly, "No."
Ross tilts his head, maintaining his gaze on Rachel. Rachel looks away slightly, her arms remaining crossed.
Ross asks again, "You still love me?"
Rachel looks at him.
Rachel says, "Yeah. So?" Ross replies, "You love me?"
Ross says softly, "No," shaking his head slightly.
Rachel shifts her gaze away, keeping her arms crossed. Ross stares at her, his posture still.
Ross gestures slightly with one hand and asks, "What does this mean?"
Ross continues, asking softly, "Do you want to get back together?"
Rachel says, "No."
Rachel pauses and adds, "Maybe."
Rachel uncrosses her arms.
Rachel clasps her hands together, saying, "I don't know."
Ross exhales audibly.
Rachel looks at him and says, "Ross,"
Rachel touches her chest with both hands as she looks at Ross.
Rachel, holding her hands to her chest, says, "I still can't forgive you for what you did."
Rachel looks down briefly, her hands remaining on her chest.
Rachel looks up at Ross and says, "But sometimes when I'm with you, I just I feel" Ross leans slightly closer and asks softly, "what?"
Rachel looks into Ross's eyes, hesitating, her hands still near her chest.
Rachel stammers softly, "I just, I feel"
Ross steps closer to Rachel.
Ross asks softly, "I just what?"
Rachel looks at Ross.
Rachel whispers, "I feel"
Ross leans in quickly and kisses Rachel. They continue kissing until the segment ends.
Rachel and Ross continue kissing on the outdoor patio. Rachel places her hands on Ross's chest and pushes back slightly as Chandler and Joey enter the patio area.
Chandler waves his hands and says, "no, I don't care." Ross and Rachel separate and turn their heads toward Chandler.
Chandler stops walking. "I'm I'm not playing one on one strip poker with you for practice," he says.
Joey opens his hands, holding a few bills of cash.
Gesturing with his hands, Joey says, "They make cards."
Rachel exhales loudly, placing both hands on her hips.
"Good night," Rachel says.
Rachel turns and walks toward the doorway. "I'm going upstairs," she says.
Rachel walks through the doorway into the lit interior. Ross watches her go, then turns back toward Chandler and Joey.
Ross stops moving and places his hands in his pockets.
"It," Chandler says.
Chandler and Joey stare at Ross in silence.
Holding up the money, Joey says, "play strip poker for practice."
Ross looks directly at Joey. The setting changes to the exterior of a dark, two-story house at night.
Now inside a dark room, a window slides open. Phoebe climbs headfirst through the opening.
Phoebe squeezes her torso through the window frame, her long hair hanging downward as she reaches for the floor.
Stuck in the window frame, Phoebe wiggles her body and exhales sharply.
"My cancer," Phoebe says as she tumbles through the window and drops out of sight.
Phoebe lands on the floor near a potted plant. She lies still briefly, then pushes herself up onto her hands and knees.
Phoebe stands, adjusts her clothing, and whispers softly.
Phoebe picks up a green bag from the floor, shushes quietly, and walks across the dark room.
Walking past a sign reading "Beach Side Realty," Phoebe shines a small flashlight ahead of her.
Phoebe whispers, "shh," moves to a counter, and reaches into her bag.
Phoebe holds a small rectangular object, illuminating it with her flashlight.
Phoebe pulls open a refrigerator door, and the interior light spills out into the dark room.
While Phoebe looks inside the refrigerator, the main room lights suddenly turn on.
Phoebe gasps loudly and turns. An older woman in a nightgown stands in a doorway, holding a wooden coat hanger.
Phoebe holds up an ice cube tray. "I didn't want to make any noise," she says. The older woman says, "Then don't break in."
The older woman lowers the coat hanger.
"I'm sorry. What are you doing here?" the older woman asks, stepping further into the room.
"I came to fill your ice cube tray," Phoebe replies.
The older woman lowers her hands slightly.
"What?" the older woman asks.
Phoebe sets the items down on the counter.
"Okay," Phoebe says.
Phoebe turns her body fully toward the older woman.
"look," Phoebe says.
Phoebe raises a small photograph in her hand.
Holding up the photograph, Phoebe says, "I took this picture from your fridge, okay? Because I know that this is my father."
Phoebe points at the photograph. "Yeah, this is Frank Buffay. And you were standing right"
"...there next to him," Phoebe continues.
"Now, look, I deserve to know where I came from, all right?" Phoebe says, gesturing with the photograph.
"So if you can help me find my father, then you should," Phoebe says.
Phoebe lowers her hands to her sides.
"Otherwise, you're just mean," Phoebe adds.
The older woman stands entirely still, keeping her gaze on Phoebe.
"So just tell me the truth," Phoebe says.
"All right?" Phoebe asks.
The older woman takes a slow breath in.
The older woman gestures toward the photograph and says, "The man in that picture is Chuck Mangione."
Phoebe holds the photograph closer and looks at it.
"That father is" Phoebe starts to say.
The older woman shakes her head.
"no, no, that's just Chuck Mangione. I sold him a house last year," the older woman says.
The older woman looks down briefly. "And I'm very sorry, but I really don't know where your father is. And that's the"
The older woman turns her body slightly away. Phoebe lowers her gaze.
Turning back, the older woman says, "but. Um, you're right."
The older woman sets the wooden hanger down on a nearby counter.
Looking at Phoebe, the older woman says, "I think that a person should know where they come from."
"Which is why" the older woman says.
The older woman closes her eyes, breathes deeply, and looks away from Phoebe.
The older woman meets Phoebe's gaze, her mouth opening and closing slightly before she speaks. "I'm okay," she says.
The older woman maintains direct eye contact with Phoebe.
"I'm your mother," the older woman says, her volume softer than her previous line.
Phoebe stares at the older woman, her eyes wide, until the segment ends.
In a kitchen, an older blonde woman in a white nightgown stands near a small rolling cart, holding a wooden stick. She faces Phoebe, who wears a blue patterned shirt.
The older woman gestures with her free hand while speaking, "You know, I wanted to tell you yesterday, but I felt all stupid." Phoebe keeps her gaze on the woman.
The setting changes to an outdoor patio at night. Joey and Chandler stand facing Ross, who sits on a bench with his arms crossed.

Joey gestures with both hands and says, "I'm telling you, you guys are totally getting back together." Ross uncrosses his arms, gestures with his hands, and responds, "That's not true. She doesn't even know what she wants. Rachel's still mad-"

Ross continues, "-about the whole thing." Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "Okay, well, then you gotta back away, all right? You don't need that kind of hurt. Take it from a guy who's never-"
Chandler points to his own chest, finishing, "-had a long term relationship."
Ross looks up at Chandler.

Ross brings one hand to his chest, then gestures upward, saying, "I know, but I really want to go up there and finish that kiss." Bonnie enters, wearing a black bikini top and a towel around her neck; she is completely bald. Chandler turns toward her and says, "Hey,"
Bonnie says, "you guys, the water is great. You should really go in." Chandler responds, "Oh, no, thanks. I just had an M&M." Bonnie replies, "Okay."
Bonnie begins walking past the men.
Bonnie says, "Well, good night."
Ross turns his head toward her and says, "Good night."
Bonnie leans down and kisses Ross on the lips. They exchange brief words, and she walks away. Joey and Chandler stare after her, then Chandler turns forward with a flat expression.
Chandler stares straight ahead and says, "There is not one hair on that head."
Ross stands up rapidly, extending his hand, and says, "Hey, it'll grow back, right? And she's really fun, and she's cool."
Ross paces slightly, gesturing with both hands. "And I'm finally moving on. I mean, breaking up with Rachel was so-" he says, his hands shaking slightly as he trails off.
Ross exhales visibly and lowers his head.
Ross gestures to his head, saying, "I'm finally feeling sane again." He points toward the house and continues, "And now if I go up there and I kiss her and God, I want to kiss-"

Ross continues, "-her and it doesn't work out, right?" He taps his chest with both hands. "Do I really want to put myself through that again?" Joey points a finger at Ross and says, "So let me get this straight. If you go with-"

Joey continues, "-Bonnie tonight, you're doing the smart, healthy thing and moving on?" Ross nods and says, "Yeah, right." Joey claps his hands together and points to his own chest, saying, "And if you go with Rachel, Bonnie's free."
Ross stares at Joey. Chandler and Joey look back at Ross in silence.
The setting changes to a hallway inside a house. Ross stands still in the center of the hallway, facing forward, between two closed doors.
Ross turns to his left and steps toward the door. He grasps the door handle and pushes the door open.
Ross leans into the open doorway and says, "Hi."
The screen turns completely black.
The setting changes to a room with slatted doors. Chandler stands outside. A woman with dark hair opens the door from the inside and smiles.
Chandler says, "there."
The dark-haired woman maintains her smile and gestures slightly with her hand.
The woman says, "That's that weird voice again."
Chandler gestures with both hands.
Chandler points at her and says, "Okay, okay. Let me try it again. You're going to want to date this next guy, I swear." He steps backward, saying, "Okay, come-"
Chandler pulls the door shut from the outside. The woman remains smiling inside.
Chandler knocks on the door again. The woman pulls it open and looks at him.
Chandler speaks with a noticeably higher-pitched vocal delivery, saying, "I can only do it with the voice now."
The woman smiles and begins pushing the door closed.
Chandler says, "Okay, okay." The woman says, "Close the door."
Chandler repeats, "Close the door," pointing at her as she shuts it.
Chandler drops to his knees outside the slatted doors. The woman opens the door again and looks down at him.
Chandler, remaining on his knees, looks up and says, "Hi."
Chandler continues, "I'm Dorf,"
Chandler maintains his gaze on the woman.
Chandler finishes, "your date for the evening."
The woman turns around and walks away into the room, leaving the door wide open. Chandler watches her go.
Chandler raises his hands and calls out, "Oh, come on. Dorf on dating. That's good stuff."
Chandler remains kneeling in the open doorway, staring into the room.
The screen turns completely black.
The screen remains completely black until the segment ends.
